goal: One-call table: every expected ROS2 topic with measured rate, frame_id, status, and per-row fix.
thoughts: rate sampling needs ≥2 s to be trustworthy — anything shorter is noise. Check QoS for Nav2-sensitive topics (sensor_data vs reliable). TF at 1 Hz usually means static publisher wiring via wrong bridge node — check_tf_health catches it. Table rows: topic | type | measured hz | expected hz | frame_id | QoS | status.
code:
topics = ros2_list_topics(include_rates=True, sample_duration_sec=2.0)

for t in topics:
    ros2_get_topic_type(topic=t.name)

check_tf_health()                                   # catches static-TF misconfigs

# Table output:
# | topic                       | type                    | hz | expected | frame_id                        | QoS         | status |
# | /scan                       | sensor_msgs/LaserScan   | 10 | 10       | laser                           | sensor_data | OK     |
# | /camera/color/image_raw     | sensor_msgs/Image       | 30 | 30       | camera_color_optical_frame      | sensor_data | OK     |
# | /odom                       | nav_msgs/Odometry       | 50 | 50       | odom                            | reliable    | OK     |
# | /tf                         | tf2_msgs/TFMessage      | 1  | ≥50     | —                               | reliable    | LOW HZ |

# Fix low /tf: re-wire robot_state_publisher via bridge node at 50 Hz.
ros2_publish(sensor_prim_path="<ROBOT_TF_SOURCE>", topic="/tf", msg_type="tf2_msgs/TFMessage", rate_hz=50)
failure_modes: Reporting rates from <1 s sample — noisy false positives
Ignoring QoS — Nav2 needs sensor_data for /scan, reliable for /tf
Skipping frame_id column — the actual bug category in 80% of bridge issues
Missing /tf_static vs /tf distinction — both matter but have different rates
